diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Core FAST criterion for stroke recognition.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden unilateral arm and leg weakness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Core FAST criterion, localising to the contralateral hemisphere.
Core FAST criterion, strongly localising to the dominant hemisphere.
The single most critical determinant of eligibility for time-limited reperfusion therapy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known atrial fibrillation with anticoagulation recently ceased

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Identifies the likely cardioembolic source given sub-therapeutic/absent anticoagulation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Power 0/5 right upper limb, 1/5 right lower limb, leftward gaze deviation, irregularly irregular pulse on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms severity, localisation, and likely cardioembolic source; clinician-only findings sealed from telehealth chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal capillary blood glucose (excludes hypoglycaemia as a stroke mimic)

investigation_details.expected_result: Normal (e.g. 5.8 mmol/L), excluding hypoglycaemia as the cause of the deficit
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Hypoglycaemia (a reversible stroke mimic) cannot be excluded; telehealth cannot measure this and it should be checked immediately on paramedic arrival, without delaying the stroke-code call.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal glucose excludes hypoglycaemia as the cause of the focal neurological deficit.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute infarction on CT/MRI brain, or exclusion of haemorrhage

investigation_details.investigation_name: Non-contrast CT head (or MRI with diffusion-weighted imaging)
investigation_details.expected_result: Acute infarction or, on CT, absence of haemorrhage supporting a diagnosis of ischaemic stroke and eligibility for thrombolysis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot distinguish ischaemic stroke from haemorrhage or safely proceed with thrombolysis without this; telehealth cannot obtain it and it must occur urgently at a stroke-capable hospital.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Required imaging to confirm the diagnosis and guide time-critical reperfusion therapy decisions.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Sudden facial droop, unilateral weakness, aphasia, and a clear last-known-well time together constitute a textbook FAST-positive stroke presentation mandating an immediate emergency stroke-code ambulance dispatch, with time being the dominant determinant of neurological outcome.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent non-contrast CT head (or MRI with diffusion-weighted imaging) and CT angiography
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the diagnosis, excludes haemorrhage, and identifies large vessel occlusion for thrombectomy eligibility; cannot be performed via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Capillary blood glucose on paramedic arrival
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Rapidly excludes hypoglycaemia as a reversible stroke mimic; should not delay the 000 call.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Outpatient neurology referral as the first step
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Grossly inappropriate given the strict time-critical thrombolysis/thrombectomy windows; this requires immediate emergency stroke-code activation, not a deferred outpatient pathway.
